title: Add podman system reset command
body:
This command will destroy all data created via podman.
It will remove containers, images, volumes, pods.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: Tests aren't hip.  One outstanding comment from mheon on tmpdir removal.  The rest LGTM.
@rhatdan looks like you might have some issues with the new reset tests....

@TomSweeneyRedHat The issue is once the storage is reset the podman varlink instance is in a weird state. Storage has been wiped out from underneath it.  We need the service to exit, so that new calls to the service will launch new instances.  The new instances will reinitialize the storage.

@vrothberg @TomSweeneyRedHat @giuseppe @mheon Finally got tests to pass, could someone merge.
